content.text:
<user_query>
look at the written report for today and give me a high level summary for each person
</user_query>

Searching the workspace for today's written report.

[REDACTED]

Summary from **`results/2026-05-20.md`** (generated today; activity window is **last week**, May 13–20).

---

### **alkamin**
Heavy work on **`anedot-one`**: prototype/UI cleanup, Turbo/CI fixes, Wrangler deploys, env config, and branch-based deploys. Also contributed to **`ui`** (app retirement, donor migration) and reviewed others’ PRs. Main theme: **greenfield web monorepo + deploy pipeline**.

### **CD-username**
Mostly **ops/support via issues**, not code: customer migrations (Texas Values, donations), commitment failures (Love & Truth), SFTP/NACHA setup, EKS/ArgoCD. Light PR review on cdktf auth build. Theme: **infrastructure & customer migration coordination**.

### **danieluhl** (you)
Split between **`anedot-one`** (initial scaffold, Hono API, Neon setup, Wrangler/deploy workflow) and **`ui`** (VT app, local dev, auth refactor, demo-table issues). Today specifically: Neon API setup and deploy-flow PRs. Theme: **new platform bootstrap + frontend/auth tooling**.

### **jin-ahn**
Issue-focused **support/infra**: migration threads (Texas Values SFTP creds, Families of Character exit), fixed Love & Truth commitment tokens, DNS review table for SFTP endpoints. One cdktf PR. Theme: **customer migrations & production incident response**.

### **jnwheeler44**
Primary contributor on **`encore`**: donation orders, billing agreements, payment intents, Rails UI controllers, Amos webhooks/charges. Also dependabot maintenance and an OTP brute-force issue. Theme: **Amos/Encore payments backend**.

### **nikkypx**
Almost entirely **PR review** (17 comment threads): approved many **Amos** changes in `anedot` and `admin` (payouts, external accounts, merchant backfill, dependency bumps). Theme: **code review gate for Amos/admin work**.

### **persianturtle**
Quiet week: **3 commits**, all in **`amos/amos-node`** updating for OpenAPI spec changes. No flagged product-facing activity. Theme: **SDK/client maintenance**.

### **rkuenneke**
One substantive PR: **Brakeman fixes + 25 spec load errors** in `admin`. Reviewed LE backfill tasks. Older S3 cleanup issue. Theme: **admin app stability & security hygiene**.

### **smoil**
Active on **`admin`** and **`anedot`**: Amos payout/external-account flows, merchant backfill, LE backfill, onboarding cleanup, SSN-hunt task, subfi_pay bumps. Mix of merges, PRs, and approvals. Theme: **Amos onboarding/payouts in admin + core app** (often as Cyrus/cyrus branches).

### **tconst**
Highest volume: **25 commits, 19 PRs** across `anedot` and `admin`. Shipped Amos payout handling, external bank accounts, payout admin views, subfi→Amos cleanup, security gem bumps, customer lookup fixes, webpack removal, and ongoing **“Amos updates”** PR today. Theme: **primary Amos integration engineer on core + admin**.

---

**Team-wide pattern:** Amos/payouts/merchant work dominated `anedot` + `admin`; parallel track on **`anedot-one`** (new UI/API stack); **`encore`** for payments backend; infra/support handled migrations and incidents.

If you want this narrowed to **only activity dated May 20** (not the full week), say so and I can filter to that.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
make a chart of the amount of work each person did
</user_query>

GitHub activity counts from **`results/2026-05-20.md`** (last week, May 13–20). Totals sum each person’s **Counts** line (commits, PRs, issues, comments, reviews).

| Person | Comments | Commits | PRs | Issues | Reviews | **Total** |
|--------|----------|---------|-----|--------|---------|-----------|
| **tconst** | 2 | 25 | 19 | — | — | **46** |
| **danieluhl** | 1 | 9 | 9 | 8 | 1 | **28** |
| **smoil** | 7 | 12 | 5 | — | — | **24** |
| **alkamin** | 4 | 10 | 9 | — | — | **23** |
| **jnwheeler44** | 2 | 12 | 3 | 1 | — | **18** |
| **nikkypx** | 17 | — | — | — | — | **17** |
| **CD-username** | 3 | — | — | 6 | — | **9** |
| **jin-ahn** | 4 | — | 1 | — | — | **5** |
| **rkuenneke** | 2 | — | 1 | 1 | — | **4** |
| **persianturtle** | — | 3 | — | — | — | **3** |

```mermaid
xychart-beta
    title "GitHub Activity Count by Person (May 13–20)"
    x-axis ["persianturtle", "rkuenneke", "jin-ahn", "CD-username", "nikkypx", "jnwheeler44", "alkamin", "smoil", "danieluhl", "tconst"]
    y-axis "Total events" 0 --> 50
    bar [3, 4, 5, 9, 17, 18, 23, 24, 28, 46]
```

**Takeaways**
- **tconst** had the most tracked events (46), mostly commits and PRs.
- **danieluhl** and **smoil** were next (28 and 24).
- **nikkypx** looks lighter on commits/PRs but had **17 comment threads** (mostly PR approvals).
- **persianturtle** had the fewest events (3 commits).

**Caveat:** Event count is a rough proxy — one large PR can represent more work than several small comments. For a visual canvas or file export, switch to Agent mode and I can generate that.

[REDACTED]
